requirements.description: Every ninth-wave child bead must map to source paths, tests or fixtures, evidence artifacts, validation commands, close reasons, pushed commits, negative controls, and claim-boundary text.

The checklist must cover incident corpus, incident replay, validation proof-memory index, operator work recommendation, operator smoothness SLO, extension resource firewall matrix, incident replay E2E evidence, source boundaries, pushed refs, and quality gates.
A passing gate may close the final gate and parent epic only after the closeout commit lands and the branch is pushed. A failing gate must emit follow-up Beads instead of optimistic closeout.

The gate must record that it does not mutate Agent Mail, RCH workers, Beads outside the normal closeout flow, runtime state, installed extensions, temp artifacts, source files, or production services.
The gate must record Python compilation, script self-test, JSON validation, git diff cleanliness, staged UBS posture, and Beads ledger reconciliation.
The closeout gate is evidence over Beads, git, docs, tests, generated artifacts, RCH, Agent Mail health, UBS, and child artifacts. It does not replace those sources of truth.
The closeout gate must not make release-facing performance, capacity, benchmark, strict drop-in, live-swarm safety, or local heavyweight Cargo fallback claims.
The ninth-wave gate may reference prior-wave authority boundaries but must not rewrite those contracts or evidence.
